sessions: session_2026-07-19-non-english-research-recovery-batch7 (batch 7, final Global-South leads + 2 small loose ends)
_meta.method: Per research-handoff-non-english.md recovery pipeline: extract named instruments from search_languages.notes -> verify via real WebSearch/WebFetch -> dedup against evidence_sources -> ingest via migration. Empties/negative findings recorded, not dropped (S6).
_meta.scope_disclosure: Two passes, per handoff S7 honest-limit clause. Batch 1: Tier-0 registry backlog (6 refs), Tier-1 richest 2 slugs by non-EN yield (deaf-classroom-reverberation-time, stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility) + a partial pass on wayfinding-dementia-spatial-design (3 of ~15 named instruments), plus the 5-language Global-South investigation. Batch 2: completed wayfinding-dementia-spatial-design (10 more instruments, Portugal re-confirmed as genuine absence), filled the NL ramp gap and corrected the ES ramp-gradient citation for stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility, and re-attempted (unsuccessfully, tooling-blocked) direct-read verification of the 9 Global-South leads. The remaining Tier-1/Tier-2 slugs (luminance-contrast-and-pattern 74 non-EN hits, sensory-room-user-control 42, cognitive-wayfinding-design/deaf-spatial-design/mental-health-built-environment/accessible-circulation-geometry 19-23 each) and the full 14-language x 24-jurisdiction sweep remain a running fraction, not claimed complete.

tier0_registry_backlog.note: Deafblind communication modalities guide (DINF, 1998). Real-retrieval verified via WebSearch (primary URL 403'd to direct fetch). Linked to deafblind-built-environment-design (DBL-09).

BCA Code on Accessibility in the Built Environment 2025, Ch.8 Signage and Wayfinding. English-language (Singapore official language) -- adds JURISDICTIONAL not LANGUAGE diversity. Linked to wayfinding-global-south (WGS-11).
DIN 18041:2016 -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00329. No new row created.
Decree 241/2017 -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00424 and REF-00459 (door provisions). Relinked REF-00424 to stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility (SRB-19) this session.
Arrete du 20 avril 2017 + CEREMA BIM guidance -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00350.

GB 50763-2012 (accessible entrances) -- registry entry is stale; already migrated as REF-00462 (exact title match: "无障碍出入口"). Relinked REF-00462 to stair-ramp-threshold-biomechanics-accessibility (SRB-16) this session.

lang_detected_hygiene_fix.criterion: standard_number-bearing rows in a single-official-language jurisdiction, whose lang_detected disagreed with that jurisdiction's language and whose pub_title/standard_number carries unambiguous native-language text or a national-standard identifier pattern (DIN/TEK17/BFS/GB/NS/SFS/JIS/UNI/Loi/Arrete/Decree/DL/DM).

deliberately_not_corrected.reason: Publisher is Dutch, but content reads as an English-language post-occupancy-evaluation report; no native-language text in title itself. Left as lang_detected=en pending direct verification.
Registry note itself hedges "likely BAGSO... brochure"; no native-language text in title. Left uncorrected.
iGlobenews international news article -- genuinely English-language reporting on a Dutch facility, not a Dutch source.
Seoul municipal guideline commonly issued bilingually; no native-language text in title. Left uncorrected.
Danish institution but title reads as English gloss with only one embedded Danish word (institution context); insufficient evidence to override.

lang_detected_hygiene_fix.jurisdiction_hygiene_applied: INTL -> INT normalization (5 rows), per db/migrations/0001-jurisdiction-hygiene.sql item 1. Item 3 (19 null jurisdictions) intentionally left untouched -- would require guessing (forbidden by S3).

additions.note: Search-notes candidate NF S31-080:2006 was verified NOT to cover classrooms (offices/tertiary only); dropped and replaced with the correct citation.

still_unresolved: JA (no legally-binding national classroom-RT standard -- genuine absence, not a gap in search)
DA (no single clean classroom-RT standard identified; Arbejdstilsynet + BR18 + DCUM cluster, needs a dedicated pass)
KO/PT (search_languages notes explicitly mark these as NOT SEARCHED for this slug)

additions_batch2.note: Height-tiered ramp table (1:12/1:16/1:20 by 0.25m/0.5m breakpoints), confirmed accurate, HIGH confidence.

additions_batch2.standard: DB-SUA Seccion SUA 1 sec.4.3 Tabla 4.1 (not SUA 9 as originally claimed)
additions_batch2.note: The search-notes-claimed ramp table (12%/8%/6%/4%) was WRONG -- it conflated the real new-construction table (10%/8%/6% + 2% cross-fall, SUA 1 sec.4.3) with a different document's existing-building tolerances (DA DB-SUA/2: 12%/10%/8%/6%). Relinked with corrected figures; the wrong table is not cited anywhere.

still_unresolved: KO (장애인 편의증진법 ramp provisions named but not cross-checked)
DA DB-SUA/2 (Spain existing-building ramp tolerance table, distinct from the corrected DB-SUA new-construction table above -- identified but not verified/ingested)

additions_batch1.standard: Helsedirektoratet Nasjonal faglig retningslinje for demens

additions_batch2.standard: Bekendtgorelse om maerkning af demensegnede plejeboliger (BEK 2243/2020)
SBi-anvisning 259 (2nd ed. 2019, year corrected from claimed 2014)
Guia de Practica Clinica sobre Alzheimer (GPC 484, 2010)
additions_batch2.note: Granular color-therapy content originally attributed to this guide actually belongs to a different Spanish publication (Fundacion Pilares/AFAD) -- ingested for confirmed general environmental-modification content only.
Chosen over the alternative candidate (4th National Dementia Plan), which was confirmed real but policy-level only with no design content.

Verified to be primarily a directory/referral portal, not a technical design standard -- ingested at tier 3 (grey), cited only for confirmed home-safety-checklist and resident-group-size content, not the more granular corridor-geometry/mirror-avoidance claims in the original search notes.

wayfinding-dementia-spatial-design.still_unresolved: COMPLETE for clearly-named instruments (batch 1 + batch 2 = 13 of ~15 originally named). Portugal (PT) independently re-checked with 5 additional search queries this session -- GENUINE ABSENCE CONFIRMED, not ingested; one unconfirmed lead flagged (IGAS 2023 ERPI Referencial de Boas Praticas, content unread, 403-blocked). One companion document flagged but not chased: the Italian 'Linee guida del Ministero della Sanita n. 1' (~1994, exact date uncertain across sources) alongside REF-00746.

SW.reasoning: Tooling reachability ruled out (Swahili content is reachable for other topics). Kenya and Tanzania both have real, current accessibility-standards regimes (Tanzania Persons with Disabilities Act 2010; Kenya Persons with Disabilities Act + National Building Code 2024 referencing KS ISO 21542:2011) but BOTH are published only in English -- no Swahili-original or Swahili-translated accessibility building standard was found despite deliberately-localized queries.

ID.verdict: (a)/(b) tooling reachability / query construction -- (c) genuine absence definitively ruled out
ID.reasoning: A mature, decades-deep Indonesian-language body of formal standards exists (SNI 03-1735-2000 since 2000; Kepmen PU 468/KPTS/1998; Permen PUPR 14/2017) plus current academic literature including a wayfinding-for-visually-disabled-students paper that matches a corpus topic slug almost verbatim -- all surfaced on the first reasonably-phrased Indonesian query.

HI.verdict: (b) query construction -- (a) tooling reachability ruled out
HI.reasoning: Native-Devanagari-script queries immediately surfaced India's Harmonised Guidelines and Space Standards for Barrier-Free Built Environment (MoHUA), including what appears to be an official Hindi translation. Romanized/naive-transliterated queries returned only English content -- a clean demonstration of the failure mode.

recovered_not_ingested.title: Hindi translation of the Harmonised Guidelines and Space Standards for Barrier-Free Built Environment
recovered_not_ingested.status: found via WebSearch (filename/title only); PDF not directly opened (403); needs independent verification before ingest

BN.verdict: mixed, leaning (b) query construction (subtler -- wrong terminology, not just wrong script)
BN.reasoning: The naive-term native-script query underperformed; switching to the Bangladesh-specific technical term "সার্বজনীনগম্য" (universal-accessibility) surfaced real content: BNBC 2020 Part 3 Appendix D (Universal Accessibility), the 2013 Rights and Protection of Persons with Disabilities Act's accessibility clause, and a dedicated Bengali-language guideline site (BUAG).

recovered_not_ingested.title: BUAG -- Bengali-language accessible-building guideline site
recovered_not_ingested.status: found via WebSearch; not directly read (proxy-blocked); publishing org not confirmed; flagged

recovered_not_ingested.title: Rights and Protection of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2013 (accessibility clause, ~Sec. 34)
recovered_not_ingested.status: found via WebSearch; not directly read; flagged

AR.verdict: (b) query construction -- (c) genuine absence firmly ruled out
AR.reasoning: Properly-phrased Modern Standard Arabic queries on niche sub-topics (wayfinding for the blind, classroom acoustics, dementia sensory design) all returned substantive relevant results immediately; a naive/garbled-transliteration query on the identical topic returned nothing useful -- a clean side-by-side demonstration. Real, current, binding national codes exist: Saudi SBC 201/901, Dubai Universal Design Code, Egyptian Code 601 (under Law 10/2018).

global_south_zero_result_investigation.tooling_caveat: Multiple independent investigating agents in batch 1 reported that direct WebFetch was blocked (HTTP 403) on a broad, seemingly unrelated set of government/NGO/standards-body domains across ALL FIVE languages' investigations, confirmed via the environment's own proxy-status endpoint as a shared infrastructure issue that session, not source-specific unavailability. This is flagged as a separate corpus-wide risk: if the ORIGINAL 81x19 multilingual sweep also depended on WebFetch (not just search) for a similar domain set, some fraction of ALL 'zero hits' (not just the 5 flagged languages) could be a tooling artifact rather than a genuine absence. Worth a dedicated audit.

batch2_direct_read_reattempt.summary: Batch 2 made a SECOND, independent attempt to directly open and read (not just WebSearch-corroborate) all 9 recovered-not-ingested candidates from batch 1 (3 Indonesian, 1 Hindi, 3 Bangladeshi, 2 of the 4 Arabic-region items -- Saudi SBC201 and Egyptian Code 601 re-attempted; Dubai/IDA not re-attempted). Result: 0/9 achieved a genuine direct read.
batch2_direct_read_reattempt.root_cause_now_precisely_diagnosed: Unlike batch 1's framing (a plausible shared-proxy issue), batch 2's investigating agents ran CONTROL-URL TESTS (fetching https://example.com, https://en.wikipedia.org, https://www.anthropic.com) and found these ALSO returned HTTP 403, and Bash curl's CONNECT tunnel was rejected for nearly every host tried. This confirms a SESSION-WIDE WebFetch tooling outage this session, not target-site blocking, not a domain-allowlist issue, and not evidence about whether the underlying sources are real (WebSearch, which uses a different retrieval path, continued to work and is the sole basis for the corroboration below).
batch2_direct_read_reattempt.one_new_finding: The Indonesian-search agent flagged a real discrepancy needing resolution before citing: SNI 03-1735-2000's actual title per fresh search snippets appears to include a fire-hazard-prevention qualifier ('...untuk Pencegahan Bahaya Kebakaran Pada Bangunan Gedung') not present in the original candidate description -- this may mean the standard's primary scope is fire-egress access, not general disability accessibility. Needs a direct read to resolve, not assumed either way.
batch2_direct_read_reattempt.recommendation: Do not attempt a third WebSearch-only verification pass on these 9 candidates -- the blocker is tooling (WebFetch outage), not insufficient searching. Retry only from a session with confirmed-functional WebFetch (verify against a trivial control URL first).

lang_detected_widened_sweep.method: Batch 1's fix was deliberately narrow (standard_number-bearing rows only). Batch 3 widened the sweep to non-standard_number rows in single-official-language jurisdictions, checking for native-language function words in pub_title.

found.note: THE ORIGINAL handoff example ('Guide acoustique pour personnes malentendantes') -- missed by batch 1 because it has no standard_number. Fixed en->fr.

lang_detected_widened_sweep.not_exhaustive: Only 2 candidates surfaced from a crude word-boundary heuristic checked against 28 non-standard_number rows in 8 jurisdictions -- a fuller audit of ALL non-standard_number rows (journal articles, reports, grey literature) across all non-English jurisdictions is still owed.

after.note: CORRECTED 2026-07-19 (batch5 adversarial doc-accuracy check): originally documented as 2; actual is 3 because the batch-3 lang_detected fix on REF-00700 ('Les espaces Snoezelen', already linked to this slug pre-session) also flipped that row to non-English (fr), a cross-effect the original count missed. Breakdown: REF-00702 (ko, pre-existing) + REF-00700 (fr, corrected in-place) + REF-00757 (es, new ingest).

sensory_room_user_control.domain_character: Unlike the acoustic/ramp/dementia slugs, most jurisdictions searched here explicitly have NO formal sensory-room design standard (an honest absence already recorded in the original search notes for JA/DA/NL/PT/FI/FR/KO/SV/ZH/DE/IT/NO) -- this domain is mostly international clinical research literature (often English-language regardless of the population studied), not national codes.

additions.note: Search notes implied this law mandates sensory rooms; verification found it creates a general procedural/dysregulation-handling duty (Art. 18), operationalized only via non-binding 2025 MINEDUC guidance, with a stricter mandate still pending as an unpassed amendment bill. Ingested for the actual statutory basis, not overclaimed as a sensory-room standard.

investigated_not_ingested.candidate: Brazil PL 3098/24 ("3 types" of sensory rooms)
investigated_not_ingested.reason: CONFIRMED to be a pending bill (Projeto de Lei), not enacted law -- cleared one legislative committee as of the most recent verifiable reporting, still needs full Chamber + Senate approval. Citing an unpassed bill as a standard would be an overclaim; deliberately not ingested. Flagged for future recheck if/when it passes.

batch3_small_followon.luminance_contrast_and_pattern_finding: This slug (74 non-EN search hits per the original handoff) was found to have ZERO source_slug_links total -- not a language gap but a slug with no evidence substrate ingested yet at all (English or otherwise). This is a different, larger problem than non-English recovery (which assumes existing EN substrate with a non-EN gap) and was NOT actioned this session -- flagged for the extraction/grading pipeline (see HANDOFF.md 'widen the grading throat'), not the equity-recovery pipeline.

luminance-contrast-and-pattern.discovery: This slug had rich TIER2 NATIVE SEARCH notes (14 languages, real named instruments) but ZERO source_slug_links at all before batch 4 -- not even English. A different failure mode than the other slugs (which had some EN coverage but zero non-EN).

after.note: 100% non-English -- every link added this batch is non-English (11 relinks of already-verified rows from earlier batches, 2 new ingests: DIN 32975 Germany, NS 11001 Norway).

mental-health-built-environment.discovery: search_languages.notes for this slug are PRE-REMEDIATION stub rows ('Extracted from BPC REF-ID table'), zero named instruments -- the notes-extraction recovery pipeline does not apply. Required fresh primary research instead, scoped to 2 jurisdictions (DE, NL).

after.note: 3 new ingests: DE EPH Planungshandbuch Psychiatrie + DGUV Information 207-027, NL Werkboek HIC. A 3rd DE lead (Niedersachsen Planungshilfe, state- not federal-level) and a 2nd NL lead (College bouw zorginstellingen, historical/defunct since ~2009) were investigated and NOT ingested.

deaf-spatial-design.discovery: Same PRE-REMEDIATION-stub situation as mental-health-built-environment. Fresh research scoped to 2 jurisdictions (DE, JA). Neither has a binding national standard for Deaf-specific spatial design (as distinct from general accessibility/acoustic codes) -- that absence is recorded honestly, not papered over.

before.note: 2 pre-existing non-EN links (REF-00346 sv, REF-00347 it) predate this session's fresh-research work -- initially undercounted in this session's own documentation as 0; corrected here per the batch-5 adversarial doc-accuracy check.

after.note: 3 new ingests: DE Deutsches Architektenblatt DeafSpace checklist article, JA Tsukuba University of Technology campus description (grey/national), JA Metote Lab (Co-1, Deaf-led, Tokyo-regional -- deliberately sought as a non-English/non-US-UK Co-1 counterweight per README.md's documented Co-1 thinness/US-UK skew).

batch5_adversarial_review.method: 4 parallel agents, blind to the ingesting agent's own reasoning, explicitly instructed to try to REFUTE rather than confirm: (1) DB-integrity + migration SQL logic audit, (2)+(3) fresh WebSearch re-verification of 8 sampled new ingests (2 batches of 4), (4) independent re-derivation of every quantitative claim in the documentation directly from the live DB.
batch5_adversarial_review.structural_integrity: Clean throughout all 4 migrations: PRAGMA foreign_key_check and integrity_check both pass, zero duplicate (slug, local_ref_id) pairs, zero orphan links, INTL->INT normalization complete.
batch5_adversarial_review.source_reverification: 7 of 8 sampled sources (REF-00757, 765, 758, 746, 754, 760, 764) CONFIRMED-ACCURATE on independent re-verification, including detailed fact-checks of specific numeric claims. 1 (REF-00751) CONFIRMED-PROBLEM -- see corrections below.

corrections.issue: Factual error: notes claimed 'max 2 units/18 residents' for Japan's MHLW dementia group-home ordinance; the current standard (post-2021-04-01 revision) actually allows up to 3 units/27 residents.
corrections.fix: notes and derivation_chain corrected; the 'strictest small-scale mandate' cross-jurisdiction superlative withdrawn (rested partly on the wrong figure).

corrections.issue: Tier doctrine violation: tiered T6 (statutory) despite being a non-mandatory GB/T recommended standard -- the row's own notes already admitted this.

evidence_type doctrine violation: formal national clinical-guideline-catalogue documents tagged evidence_type='clinical' (reserved for primary research per tier-system.md), a pairing with zero precedent among the DB's ~245 other T2/clinical-adjacent rows.
corrections.fix: evidence_type corrected to 'standard_eb' (T2's 'named-organisation evidence-based standards' category); tier=2 unchanged, it is the correct rank.

corrections.issue: Tier + evidence_type doctrine violation: a Ministry-of-Environment housing design guide (not a clinical document) was tiered T2/clinical, granting undeserved best-practice-anchoring status.
Tier + evidence_type doctrine violation: the row's OWN notes already said this is welfare/communication-modality documentation, not a design standard -- yet it was tiered T2/clinical.
corrections.fix: tier corrected 2->3, evidence_type corrected clinical->grey, matching how this session tiered directly-comparable institutional sources elsewhere (REF-00755, REF-00763, REF-00764).

corrections.issue: Co-1 encoding violation: deliberately ingested AS Co-1 lived-experience evidence (co1_provenance/co1_source_type set) but placed at tier=3/evidence_type=grey instead of this DB's established Co-1 convention (tier=1/evidence_type=co1, used by 29 of 30 pre-existing co1 rows) -- silently defeating the batch's own stated purpose (a non-English, non-US/UK Co-1 counterweight), since nothing querying evidence_type='co1' would have found it.

Likely lang_detected over-correction (batch 1): flipped en->sv, but Lund University's own LUP publications catalogue lists this dissertation under an English title, and Nordic medical-faculty dissertations are commonly English despite a Swedish subtitle in secondary citations -- the same ambiguous-case pattern batch 1 correctly left uncorrected for 5 other rows.

verification_note honesty defect (data value NOT wrong): stated justification claimed 'verified from native-language text' but both pub_title and standard_number fields are entirely English -- the actual basis was jurisdiction-inference, not text in the row itself. The language VALUE (no) was independently re-confirmed correct.
corrections.fix: verification_note corrected for audit-trail honesty; lang_detected value unchanged.

flagged_not_fixed.issue: Relink cites a stair/tactile-contrast provision of GB 50763 that lives in a different section than this row's own excerpt (accessible entrances). Same real standard, section-level imprecision, not a topical mismatch or fabrication.
flagged_not_fixed.action: Annotated in source_slug_links.relevance_note; not further corrected -- would need a section-specific GB 50763 row that may not exist in the DB yet.

documentation_corrections.reason: missed cross-effect: a batch-3 lang_detected fix on an already-linked row (REF-00700) also raised this slug's non-EN tally.
undercounted 2 pre-existing non-EN relinks (REF-00346 sv, REF-00347 it) that predated this session's fresh-research additions.

batch6_global_south_resolution.summary: Follow-on session after PR #19 merged. WebFetch confirmed non-functional for a 3rd consecutive session (control-URL test). Applied the batch-5-validated WebSearch-corroboration + VERIFIED-2 methodology to the 9 flagged leads instead of continuing to wait for WebFetch.

ingested.note: Mandatory via Royal Decree M/43. First Saudi jurisdiction in this corpus.
Mandatory via ministerial decree + Law 10/2018. Base edition 2003 anchored; later update years uncertain, not guessed. First Egyptian jurisdiction in this corpus.
Mandatory via Dubai Municipality permitting + Wosool certification (Oct 2023). First UAE/Gulf jurisdiction in this corpus.

ingested.candidate: Bangladesh Rights and Protection of Persons with Disabilities Act 2013, Section 34
ingested.confidence: high on existence, medium-high on enforceability characterization
ingested.note: INGESTED WITH HONEST CAVEAT: multiple independent sources describe Section 34's enforcement as weak/aspirational (soft 'as much as possible' phrasing, no designated enforcement authority) -- tiered T6 because it is genuinely statute, not because it is strongly enforced. First Bangladeshi jurisdiction in this corpus.

excluded_after_careful_recheck.reason: Full official title retrieved this batch: 'Tata Cara Perencanaan Akses Bangunan dan Akses Lingkungan UNTUK PENCEGAHAN BAHAYA KEBAKARAN pada Bangunan Gedung' -- this is a FIRE-SAFETY EGRESS standard (access roads/openings for firefighting), not a disability-accessibility standard. The original lead was based on a truncated title fragment that omitted the fire-hazard-prevention qualifier. Correctly excluded, not ingested.

Could not be re-located via WebSearch this batch (4 distinct queries, including exact filename, all empty). Relationship to already-ingested REF-00509 (India HGS-2021) remains undetermined -- could be a translation of REF-00509, a translation of a DIFFERENT older 2016 document, or unconfirmable. Not ingested to avoid double-counting or citing an unretrievable file.

Confirmed to be a derivative/secondary Bengali-language explainer of BNBC 2020 Part 3 Appendix D, not an independent primary standard. Operating/publishing organization remains unconfirmed after 2 separate investigation rounds (batch 1 and batch 6). Not citable as a primary source.

batch6_global_south_resolution.still_unresolved_not_reinvestigated_this: RESOLVED in batch 7 -- see batch7_global_south_closeout below.
batch6_global_south_resolution.webfetch_status: Confirmed non-functional via control-URL test (example.com returned HTTP 403) for the 3rd consecutive session (batches 1-2, batch 4, batch 6). Recommend escalating to the project owner as a likely persistent environment/infrastructure issue rather than continuing to re-test it each session.

batch7_global_south_closeout.summary: Follow-on session after PR #20 (batch 6) merged. WebFetch confirmed non-functional for a 4th consecutive session (control-URL test). Closed out the 2 Indonesian leads batch 6 left untouched, plus 2 small unrelated loose ends (Italian date, Portugal IGAS content), all via the batch-5/6-validated WebSearch-corroboration + VERIFIED-2 methodology.

ingested.candidate: Permen PUPR No. 14/PRT/M/2017 (Persyaratan Kemudahan Bangunan Gedung)
ingested.confidence: high on identity/title/scope; medium on current-force status (JDIH BPK RI 'Berlaku' status confirmed via WebSearch snippet, not a direct page read)
ingested.note: Complete official title checked against the SNI 03-1735-2000 scope-trap lesson from batch 6 -- no hidden qualifier found, genuinely about building accessibility. Linked to wayfinding-global-south as WGS-16. First Indonesian jurisdiction in this corpus.

excluded_after_careful_recheck.reason: Complete official title confirmed genuinely disability-accessibility (no scope trap), but explicitly revoked by Permen PU No. 30/PRT/M/2006 per that regulation's own JDIH BPK RI text ('dicabut dan dinyatakan tidak berlaku', effective 1 December 2006). Superseded regulations are not ingested as active citations per this project's code-currency discipline.

small_loose_ends_resolved.item: Italian companion document date on REF-00746 ('Linee guida del Ministero della Sanita n. 1')
small_loose_ends_resolved.resolution: Confirmed 31 marzo 1994 (not '31 gennaio 1994' as previously uncertain), via multiple convergent independent WebSearch sources. A separate, genuine 31 maggio 1994 date is the transmission date to the Regions, likely the source of the original date-confusion. Legge 23 dicembre 1994, n. 724 art. 3 cross-reference now confirmed, not just suspected. REF-00746's notes/derivation_chain updated; the companion document itself remains un-ingested (organizational/managerial content, not structural).

small_loose_ends_resolved.item: Portugal IGAS 2023 ERPI 'Referencial de Boas Praticas' -- dementia-specific content check
small_loose_ends_resolved.resolution: No dementia-specific built-environment/design content found. Confirms (does not overturn) Portugal's existing genuine-absence finding for wayfinding-dementia-spatial-design. The document is clinical/organizational health-care guidance co-authored with medical/nursing/pharmacy professional orders, no architectural body involved. Physical/spatial specifications surfaced in early searches traced to a different document (Portaria n. 349/2023, the ERPI licensing regulation), not this referencial. No DB change made.

batch7_global_south_closeout.outcome: All 9 original Global-South leads (batches 1-7) now resolved: 5 ingested (Saudi Arabia, Egypt, UAE, Bangladesh, Indonesia), 4 correctly excluded (Indonesia x2 -- SNI wrong-scope + Kepmen superseded; Bangladesh's BUAG -- secondary/unconfirmed; India's Hindi MoHUA PDF -- unlocatable). Swahili's genuine-absence finding stands unchanged.
batch7_global_south_closeout.webfetch_status: Confirmed non-functional (WebSearch-only used throughout) for a 4th consecutive session (batches 1-2, batch 4, batch 6, batch 7). Recommend escalating to the project owner as a likely persistent environment/infrastructure issue.
